pr.title: Match and link ftlinkspecialn via hacky solution, cleanup inline names
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Made Link's neutral special source, ftLk_SpecialN.c, build as a Matching object and therefore link into GALE01. The source-side fix was explicitly described as hacky: it cleaned up placeholder inline helper names while also using codegen-sensitive tactics such as duplicate zero Vec3 constants, stack padding macros, helper splitting, and literal/ordering changes. The symbols file was also adjusted heavily for local/global scope and BSS layout, including baselib/devcom data. decomp.dev reported +1616 matched code bytes, +6500 linked code bytes, and +33024 matched data bytes, with new matches for ftLk_SpecialNStart_Anim, ftLk_SpecialAirNStart_Anim, and devcom BSS/SBSS.

key_files.role: Refactored Link neutral-B helpers and applied matching hacks for animation/collision codegen.
Changed ftLk_SpecialN.c from Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching).
Updated local/global symbol scopes, added local section labels, renamed ftLk zero constants, and split devcom BSS objects.

what_changed: configure.py now lists melee/ft/chara/ftLink/ftLk_SpecialN.c as Matching instead of NonMatching.
ftLk_SpecialN.c replaced placeholder/generic helpers such as unkCondition and inlineA* with more descriptive helpers: isDrawback, isDrawn, animate, animate_nopad, doEndColl, and doAirColl.
Moved static MotionFlags constants mf and coll_mf near the top of ftLk_SpecialN.c and reused them for motion-state transitions.
Added three separate static const Vec3 zero constants, zero/zero2/zero3, and used them to initialize lpos/rpos/root in the Link bow animation path.
Split identical-looking animation logic into animate, which includes FORCE_PAD_STACK_16 and FORCE_PAD_STACK_8 padding, and animate_nopad, which avoids that padding for loop callsites.

Reshaped ftLk_SpecialNStart_Anim and ftLk_SpecialAirNStart_Anim so they inline command-variable handling, call isDrawn, update arrow positioning through animate, and transition to loop motion states when frames finish.

Reshaped collision helpers: setupParts and updateParts became static inline, doEndColl was moved and padded, doAirColl was extracted before air collision wrappers, and transition literals were normalized to 1.0f/0.0f.

symbols.txt changed many compiler-local symbols from global to local, added labels such as ...rodata.0, ...data.0, and ...bss.0, renamed ftLk SpecialN rodata constants to zero/zero2/zero3, and split HSD_DevCom_804C6330 into HSD_DevCom_804C6330 plus HSD_DevCom_804C6330_bufs and fileinfo.
smart_moves: Used helper splitting instead of forcing one shared helper everywhere: animate and animate_nopad preserve readable intent while allowing different stack layouts at different callsites.
Kept duplicate zero Vec3 constants rather than deduplicating them, matching the emitted rodata layout and symbol references needed by the object.
Combined source-level codegen shaping with symbol-scope cleanup before flipping the configure.py object state to Matching.
Renamed helpers from inlineA*/unkCondition to action-oriented names, making the decomp more searchable without claiming exact struct field meanings that are still unknown.
Localized compiler-emitted or file-private symbols in symbols.txt, reducing accidental global exports and enabling data matches outside the ftLink source itself.
decomp_lessons: A mostly matched fighter file may still require non-semantic stack/layout work before it can be linked; FORCE_PAD_STACK and local variable lifetime/order can be decisive.
Do not prematurely deduplicate identical constants in decomp code: separate static const objects like zero, zero2, and zero3 may be required to match rodata identity and ordering.
When one inline helper matches one callsite but not another, create explicit padded and unpadded variants rather than hiding the difference in less readable control flow.
Flipping an object from NonMatching to Matching can require symbols.txt maintenance beyond that object, especially local/global scope corrections and BSS object splitting.
If field semantics are uncertain, preserve placeholder field names such as cmd_unk* but improve helper names around observed behavior, such as isDrawn and isDrawback.
Explicit float literals, boolean false, helper declaration order, and static const placement can all affect MWCC output enough to matter for small final matches.
naming_conventions: Boolean helpers use is* predicate names, shown by isDrawback and isDrawn.
Use descriptive action helpers like animate, doEndColl, and doAirColl instead of placeholder inlineA* names once behavior is recognizable.
Use a suffix such as _nopad to document a matching-only stack-layout variant, as in animate_nopad.
Short local MotionFlags constants were named mf and coll_mf, with coll_mf indicating collision-state transition flags.
When the only proven meaning is codegen/layout, plain names like zero, zero2, and zero3 are preferable to invented semantic names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied FORCE_PAD_STACK_16, FORCE_PAD_STACK_8, and FORCE_PAD_STACK_4 to influence stack frame layout.
Initialized Vec3 locals from three distinct static const Vec3 objects to preserve separate rodata entries.
Split animation helper code into padded and unpadded inline variants for callsite-specific matching.
Used explicit 1.0f and 0.0f transition arguments and false boolean assignments where the diff showed literal normalization.
Reordered static constants and helper definitions so emitted data/code aligned with expected output.
Adjusted symbols.txt scopes from global to local for file-private callbacks, strings, floats, BSS, SBSS, and SData symbols.
Added local section labels such as ...rodata.0, ...data.0, and ...bss.0 to match section starts.
Split HSD_DevCom_804C6330 BSS layout into a 0x10 global object, an 0x8000 local buffer object, and a local fileinfo object.
review_feedback: No human PR body, review comments, or reviews were present in the slice; intent is inferred from the title, diff, and bot report.
decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 matched code at 55.88% (+0.04%, +1616 bytes), linked code at 32.28% (+0.17%, +6500 bytes), matched data at 37.51% (+2.73%, +33024 bytes), and linked data +52 bytes.
decomp-dev[bot] listed 4 new matches: main/sysdolphin/baselib/devcom .bss, ftLk_SpecialAirNStart_Anim, ftLk_SpecialNStart_Anim, and main/sysdolphin/baselib/devcom .sbss.
